article_meta.title: San Francisco sues 10 major food manufacturers over ultra-processe

reasoning: Wrong-direction classification with overconfident score and multiple attribution-rule violations. The assistant leans on quoted industry statements as evidence and misreads structural emphasis in a largely balanced, neutral news report.

custom_reasoning.classification_accuracy: The article reads Center (at most Lean Left due to supportive link headlines and inclusion of CDC/science context). Labeling it Lean Right is the wrong direction.
custom_reasoning.confidence_calibration: 0.8 implies a clear lean; the piece shows mixed sourcing and neutral attributions, so confidence should be around 0.5–0.7 or lower.
custom_reasoning.reasoning_evidence_quality: Key indicators repeatedly quote attributed sources (industry statements) and treat them as authorial evidence, violating the attribution rule; also incorrectly claims the author gives the last word to business.
